line_items.description: Remove Laminated - comp. shingle rfg. - w/out felt
line_items.f9: Our line item 1 covers insurance lines 1 and 6. We are requesting an additional 0.01 SQ for Remove Laminated - comp. shingle rfg. - w/out felt, bringing our total to 48.18 SQ. Per the EagleView report, the measured roof area confirms this quantity. Please see the attached EagleView for verification of the roof measurements.
ins_carrier_notes: Age/Life: 10/30 yrs, Avg. Dep%: 33.33%. Tax: 534.59. Component RFG300 from this line item was priced by ITEL Asphalt Shingle Pricing (ASP) on 20 Mar 2026. Auto Calculated Waste: 6.6%, 3.16SQ. Options: Valleys: Closed-cut (half laced), Include eave starter course: No, Include rake starter course: No, Exposure - Hip/Valley/Starter: 5 5/8". Bundle Rounding: 0.5%, 0.23SQ - (included in waste calculation above)

line_items.f9: Our line item 2 corresponds to insurance line 2 for drip edge. We are in agreement with the insurance quantity of 446.0 LF. The EagleView report reflects a base measurement of 427.16 LF for eave and rake edges; the requested 446.0 LF accounts for all applicable edges including any areas not captured in the automated measurement. Drip edge must be replaced in full per manufacturer requirements and local code when a new roof system is installed, and we defer to the agreed quantity of 446.0 LF as reflected in the insurance estimate.

Our line item 3 covers insurance line 3. We are requesting an additional 0.01 SQ for Roofing felt - 15 lb., bringing our total to 48.18 SQ. The felt underlayment must be installed across the full measured roof area of 48.18 SQ as confirmed by the attached EagleView report. Please see the attached EagleView for verification of the roof measurements.

Our line item 5 covers insurance line 5. We are in agreement with the insurance quantity of 446.0 LF for Asphalt starter - universal starter course. The starter course is required along all eave and rake edges of the roof, and the 446.0 LF quantity is consistent with the drip edge perimeter confirmed by the attached EagleView report.

line_items.description: R&R Hip / Ridge cap - High profile - composition shingles
R&R Hip / Ridge cap - High profile - composition shingles
line_items.f9: Our line item 7 covers insurance line 7. We are requesting an additional 82.88 LF for Hip / Ridge cap - Standard profile - composition shingles, bringing our total to 198.0 LF. Per the EagleView report, the total measured ridges and hips equal 198.25 LF, which supports our requested quantity of 198.0 LF. The insurance estimate of 115.12 LF significantly undercounts the actual ridge and hip length on this roof. Please see the attached EagleView for confirmation of the measurements.

The insurance report did not include Prime & paint roof jack. We are requesting 7.0 EA for this item. When roof jacks and pipe boots are replaced during a storm restoration, the exposed metal flanges require priming and painting to prevent rust and ensure a proper finish that matches pre-loss condition. Please see the attached photo report for documentation of the roof penetrations requiring this work.

Detach & Reset Power attic vent cover only - plastic
Remove Additional charge for steep roof - 7/12 to 9/12 slope
Additional charge for steep roof - 7/12 to 9/12 slope
Remove Additional charge for steep roof greater than 12/12 slope
Additional charge for steep roof greater than 12/12 slope
Remove Additional charge for high roof (2 stories or greater)
Additional charge for high roof (2 stories or greater)

line_items.f9: The insurance report did not include step flashing. We are requesting 66.0 LF of step flashing as confirmed by the EagleView report, which shows 66.5 LF of step flashing on this roof. During the roof tear-off process, the existing step flashing is disturbed and bent back, making it impossible to reuse without compromising the weather-tight seal between the roof deck and the adjacent wall. New step flashing must be installed per manufacturer requirements and proper roofing standards. Please see the attached EagleView report for confirmation of the measurements.

The insurance report did not include counterflashing - apron flashing. We are requesting 59.0 LF of apron flashing for this roof. During the roof tear-off process, the existing apron flashing is disturbed and cannot be reinstalled to manufacturer specifications, requiring full replacement to maintain a proper weather-tight seal between the roof deck and the adjacent wall or structure. New apron flashing is a necessary component of a code-compliant and watertight roof system. Please see the attached EagleView report and photo documentation for confirmation.

line_items.description: R&R Gutter / downspout - aluminum - up to 5"
line_items.f9:
The Insurance report left out the R&R Gutter / downspout - aluminum - up to 5".
We are requesting 50.0 LF.
1. Xactimate total cost is $623.90.
   a. Please see attached Photo Report showing damage to the r&r gutter / downspout - aluminum - up to 5".

The insurance report did not include the fence work covered under our Grizzly Fence & Patio sub bid. We are requesting approval of our full sub bid totaling $2,143.83. The attached Grizzly Fence & Patio bid details the scope of work required, including 1,434 LF of fencing affected by the storm event. Please see the attached sub contractor bid for full pricing and scope details.

line_items.f9: The insurance report did not include the window screen replacement covered under our FoGlass Window LLC sub bid. We are requesting approval of our full sub bid totaling $1,012.83 for window screens on the front, right, and left elevations. Hail impact causes direct damage to window screens, including bent frames and torn mesh that cannot be repaired to pre-loss condition. Please see the attached FoGlass Window LLC bid and photo report documenting the hail damage to the window screens for review.

line_items.description: Haul debris - per pickup truck load - including dump fees

line_items.f9: This project requires general contractor oversight and coordination of multiple trades, including roofing, fencing, and window work. We are requesting overhead and profit at the standard 20% rate applied to subcontractor costs, as our company serves as the general contractor and single point of contact responsible for vetting, scheduling, managing, and warranting all subcontractors involved in the restoration. This coordination role carries direct liability for workmanship and warranty across all trades, which is consistent with standard industry practice and Xactimate guidelines for projects involving general contractor services. Overhead and profit is not a duplicate charge but rather compensation for the administrative, supervisory, and liability functions that a general contractor performs above and beyond the scope of any individual trade.
